title: Punjabi Tawa Chicken Tikka

ingredients.name: boneless chicken thighs or breast, cut in 4 cm pieces

subs.name: paneer (300 g) or halloumi cut in thick slices; reduce cooking time to 2 minutes per side
coconut yogurt for a dairy-free marinade; the result will be slightly different in flavour
any wide heavy pan or grill pan gives good char; a regular non-stick pan will cook but not char as well
ghee for a richer, more traditional basting flavour

steps.text: Combine the curd, ginger-garlic paste, red chilli powder, garam masala, cumin, turmeric, lemon juice, 1 tbsp oil and salt in a bowl. Make 2–3 shallow cuts in each chicken piece so the marinade penetrates. Add the chicken and turn to coat thoroughly; marinate for at least 30 minutes at room temperature or up to 4 hours in the fridge.

Heat a tawa or flat skillet over high flame until very hot. Add the remaining oil. Lay the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer without overlapping. Do not move them for 3–4 minutes until dark grill marks form on the underside.
Flip the chicken and cook the other side for 4–5 minutes until charred in spots and the juices run clear when you pierce a thick piece. Lower the flame to medium if the marinade is burning too fast.
Spread the butter over the chicken in the last minute and let it melt and sizzle into the pieces. Remove from the tawa and rest for 2 minutes.
Arrange on a platter with raw onion rings and lemon wedges. Squeeze lemon over the tikka and serve immediately as a starter or with mint chutney and naan.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: curd → coconut yogurt for a dairy-free marinade; the result will be slightly different in flavour; chicken → paneer (300 g) or halloumi cut in thick slices; reduce cooking time to 2 minutes per side; oil → any wide heavy pan or grill pan gives good char; a regular non-stick pan will cook but not char as well; butter → ghee for a richer, more traditional basting flavour
